w:r.w:t: House No 103, Housing Board Colony, Hatkeshar, Dhamtari, Chhattisgarh 493773
Registrar of Firms and Societies Chhattisgarh
“Working with people, never for them. We build self-reliant rural communities across Chhattisgarh through education, environmental conservation, and sustainable livelihoods.”
Welcome to the official YouTube channel of
, a nonprofit organization founded in 2011. We are dedicated to empowering rural communities in Chhattisgarh, India, through impactful initiatives aligned with the

. Our work focuses on promoting clean water access, quality education, holistic health, cultural preservation, and sustainable development, positively impacting millions of lives. Subscribe to explore inspiring stories, educational content, and real-life transformations as we work together to build a sustainable, prosperous future for all. Join us in making a difference!

Bolti Nadi Initiative was set up in 2016 by the core team of the ‘Meer Foundation’ Meer Foundation is a registered non-government-organization established in 2011, working in the areas of environment with the mission of making rural Chhattisgarh drought-free and prosperous. The health of our river and streams depends on all of us,

mission is to protect the rivers and streams that provide drinking water for more than 20 million people across the state. Working across the basin to reduce pollution, protect headwaters and promote water-smart practices and policies.

Embark on a transformative journey with Yoga Kutumb, an initiative ardently supported by Meer Foundation. Aligned with the ethos of promoting yoga globally, Yoga Kutumb endeavors to integrate the principles of holistic well-being into everyday life. Mirroring the Indian government's commitment to making yoga an intrinsic part of our daily routines, our initiative aspires to create a culture where mind, body, and soul converge in harmony. Beyond a mere exercise routine, Yoga Kutumb envisions yoga as a universal language promoting a balanced and healthy lifestyle. With certified yoga professionals leading the way, our mission extends to sharing India's ancient wisdom and cultural heritage, fostering a global community that embraces the transformative power of yoga. Join us on this holistic journey towards enhanced well-being and unity.

Embark on a journey through time with "Local Heritage," a heartfelt initiative by Meer Foundation. Every year on World Heritage Day (International Day for Monuments and Sites) on 18th April, we invite youth worldwide to dedicate one hour of their leisure time to stroll to a nearby heritage site in their city. Capture the essence of historical tales and cultural richness as we unravel the stories engraved in the stones of our own cities, preserving the legacy of our heritage. Share your journey on social media using #LocalHeritage and join us in celebrating the profound beauty of our rich cultural tapestry. This initiative is a step towards fostering a deep connection with our roots and building a shared appreciation for the cultural heritage that binds us all.

is a transformative initiative spearheaded by Meer Foundation, a nonprofit organization founded in 2011 in Chhattisgarh, India. This groundbreaking endeavor is dedicated to reshaping the educational landscape in rural Chhattisgarh, recognizing the vast potential of education and offering high-quality experiences tailored for the region's most underserved students. As part of Meer Foundation's commitment to fostering educational equity,

bridges the gap between traditional learning and modern educational methodologies. The initiative emphasizes academic excellence while advocating for universal access to the best educational resources, ensuring that no student is left behind in the evolving digital age.

Rooted in the belief that educated individuals with empathy can drive transformative change, Meer Foundation is dedicated to improving the lives of impoverished communities in Chhattisgarh, India. Focusing on the most vulnerable regions, the foundation organizes collectives, particularly empowering women to secure dignified livelihoods and access government programs. By recruiting professionals with diverse expertise and a commitment to empathy, Meer Foundation prioritizes skill development and initiatives within communities rather than imposing solutions. This approach aims to instill agency, leading to comprehensive positive changes in social, psychological, and economic conditions. The foundation collaborates with civil society and the government to influence development policies, envisioning a future where rural communities in Chhattisgarh thrive with self-determination and engagement with the world around them.

In a momentous display of patriotism and unity, Meer Foundation, led by Founder Amir Hashmi, played a pivotal role in achieving a historic milestone during the 'Meri Jaan Tiranga Hai' rally in Raipur, Chhattisgarh, on August 11, 2019. As a core partner with
Foundation, Meer Foundation actively participated in forming a

human chain, symbolizing national unity. The rally, a unique expression of love for the tricolor and the nation, featured diverse participants, including school students, CRPF personnel, police, NGOs, and locals. Amir Hashmi's leadership in hosting the tricolor world record added a significant dimension, highlighting Meer Foundation's commitment to fostering patriotism. The event encompassed cultural enrichment with patriotic expressions by students, chants of unity, and collaboration with over 40 NGOs, showcasing a collective effort for success. This historic achievement, a

tricolor flag, set during the rally, became a symbol of national pride, unity, and love for the country. Meer Foundation takes immense pride in contributing to this historic event, aligning with the spirit of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) by fostering patriotism, unity, and community participation.
The International Journal of Multidisciplinary Explication and Emerging Research (IJMEER)
SDG 4: Quality Education (Inclusive and Equitable Quality Education)
SDG 9: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure (Research and Innovation Promotion)
SDG 10: Reduced Inequalities (Equitable Access to Knowledge)
SDG 16: Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions (Governance and Policy Research)
SDG 17: Partnerships for the Goals (Global Academic Collaboration)
International Journal of Multidisciplinary Explication and Emerging Research (IJMEER)

is a quarterly, open-access, peer-reviewed academic journal published by Meer Foundation, Dhamtari (Chhattisgarh), established in 2026 to promote interdisciplinary research and global scholarly exchange. Operating in both online and print formats, the journal publishes original research articles, reviews, and case studies across more than 70 academic disciplines, with English as the medium of publication. IJMEER follows a structured double-blind peer review system, including plagiarism screening, expert evaluation, and editorial decision-making, with an average publication timeline of 4–8 weeks. Each issue is systematically organized with proper bibliographic details and DOI assignment for individual articles, ensuring academic credibility, citation tracking, and indexing compatibility. The journal maintains quarterly issues, transparent editorial processes, and an open-access model without subscription barriers, supported by a defined Article Processing Charge structure. With dedicated sections such as editorial board, archives, author guidelines, and publication policies, IJMEER aligns with standard ISSN requirements for online serials and functions as a scholarly publishing initiative of Meer Foundation to facilitate accessible, structured, and quality research dissemination.

The IJMEER journal initiative aligns with key Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) by promoting accessible knowledge, research dissemination, and interdisciplinary academic collaboration. The most relevant SDGs include:
through open-access research and academic learning;
by supporting innovation and research ecosystems;
by encouraging inclusive and diverse scholarly participation;
through research in law, governance, and policy; and
by fostering global academic collaboration and knowledge exchange.
